title: centaurea rhenana subsp. savranica
intro: Centaurea rhenana subsp. savranica, also known as Savran knapweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. This perennial herb is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea rhenana subsp. savranica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat minor ailments and promote overall well-being.
